texto: Everything since the beginning of time was working together to make my happiness possible: and then you. You walked into the audiovisual lab in your flannel shirt...and you fucked it up! You fucked everything up! Do you understand that? Because of you, the entire universe is ruined...forever!"--Kari, The Pavilion
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Craig Wright que provoca passagem do tempo e prioridades. Ao questionar “Everything since the beginning of time was working together to make my happiness possible: and th…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre amor, destiny, disappointment, com foco em trabalho e disappointment.

frase_original: Everything since the beginning of time was working together to make my happiness possible: and then you. You walked into the audiovisual lab in your flannel shirt...and you fucked it up! You fucked everything up! Do you understand that? Because of you, the entire universe is ruined...forever!"--Kari, The Pavilion

texto: At every single moment, the whole creation is beginning again, stretching the tent of the present moment to bursting. And the waves that push up through the oceans, and the waves that push up through the stars; and the waves that push upwards through history are the same waves that push up through us. And so we have to say yes to time, even though it means speeding forward into memory; forgetfulness; and oblivion. Say “no” to time; hold on to what you were or what she was; hold onto the past, even out of love... and I swear it will tear you to shreds. This universe will tear you to shreds.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Craig Wright sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “At every single moment, the whole creation is beginning again, stretching the tent of the present…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, vida, love, útil em momentos de love e time.

And the waves that push up through the oceans, and the waves that push up through the stars; and the waves that push upwards through history are the same waves that push up through us. And so we have to say yes to time, even though it means speeding forward into memory; forgetfulness; and oblivion. Say “no” to time; hold on to what you were or what she was; hold onto the past, even out of love... and I swear it will tear you to shreds. This universe will tear you to shreds.
